response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, senior full-stack engineer with genuine systems-programming credentials (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second, embedded and desktop work) and a strong mentoring record. However, the role's core minimum qualification is 8 years of C++ experience, and his professional C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project; his systems work is predominantly DLang and Node/TypeScript. His infrastructure experience is also application-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployments) rather than building large-scale compute platforms, and he has not held a formal technical-leadership role directing cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second)
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing record: workshops, multi-module courses, conference talks, and maintained open-source test frameworks
Broad engineering range across web, mobile, embedded, and DevOps, with a craftsmanship focus (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8 years of C++ — professional C++ use is limited to a ~7-month stint; primary systems language is DLang
No formal technical-leadership or team-lead role; leadership evidence is limited to mentoring, workshops, and founding a small open-source project
Cloud experience is consuming/deploying cloud services rather than designing production cloud compute architecture at platform scale
